metadata.source: Per-region climate.md dossiers + national/regional climate-projection sources (CMIP6 SSP2-4.5, IPMA, AEMET, Meteo-France DRIAS, Met Office UKCP18, NCA5, Ouranos, WICCI, etc.)
metadata.source_url: data/research-dossier/<id>/climate.md (intermediate); see per-feature source_url for primary
metadata.license: Research dossiers internal; underlying climate-projection sources are typically open government / publicly funded
metadata.note: Delivers Overview decision 8.1's 'climate-buffering features (altitude, distance-to-coast, mountain-shelter exposure)' as a structured per-jurisdiction layer. Pairs the static buffering features with the dynamic trajectory_under_warming so the layer surfaces both 'how resilient is the microclimate' and 'how fast is it being eroded.'

properties.buffering_notes: Dossier describes Alentejo as one of the hotter, drier corners of Europe with no buffering features named in climate.md.
properties.primary_vulnerability_signal: extreme summer heat and drought
properties.trajectory_notes: Projected +2.7 to 2.9C warming envelope per IPMA, with summer warming of +2.0 to 2.5C and 10 to 15% reduction in wet-day frequency by 2041 to 2060.
properties.source: WorldClim CMIP6 v2.1 (2024); Soares et al. Scientific Reports 2024; IPMA Climate Scenarios 2023; Gomes et al. 2024
properties.gaps: No explicit altitude band or microclimate-buffering feature discussed.

properties.buffering_notes: Climate.md identifies Galicia as a partial climate refugium in IPCC AR6, buffered against extreme heat by Atlantic maritime influence.
properties.primary_vulnerability_signal: more intense winter storms and AMOC-shift tail risk
properties.trajectory_notes: Projected +1.5 to 2.0C by mid-century, more modest than Iberian interior; summer heat unlikely to become a primary stress this century.
properties.source: WorldClim CMIP6 v2.1 (2024); AEMET 2023; MDPI Land 14:85 (2025)
properties.gaps: No specific altitude range given for coastal versus interior siting.

properties.buffering_notes: Climate.md cites elevation, forest cover, and continental setting as providing partial buffering; high-elevation Carpathian valleys are EEA-flagged refugia.
properties.primary_vulnerability_signal: forest-pathogen and bark-beetle outbreaks in spruce monocultures
properties.trajectory_notes: Summer warming of +2.5 to 3.0C by 2050 over 1961-1990 baseline; heatwave frequency in Romanian lowlands roughly tripled since 1990s with uplands trending similarly.
properties.source: WorldClim CMIP6 v2.1 (2024); Bojariu et al. Springer 2014; World Bank CCKP Romania
properties.gaps: Plateau-versus-Carpathian-flank altitude bands not differentiated in detail.

properties.buffering_notes: Climate.md describes Atlantic-maritime climate that drifts rather than transforms with no projected heat-stress regime change; water.md and soil.md document blanket peat storing 240 to 3000+ t C/ha with order-of-magnitude reliable summer flow.
properties.primary_vulnerability_signal: Atlantic storm intensification and AMOC-shift tail risk
properties.trajectory_notes: Projected +0.7 to 1.5C by mid-century, one of the slowest-warming European candidates; no projected drought, heat, or fire regime change.
properties.source: EPA Research 471 (2023); Met Eireann 2024; ICHEC 2023; WorldClim CMIP6 v2.1
properties.gaps: Altitude range for siting not specified; AMOC-shift probability not quantified.

properties.buffering_notes: Climate.md frames west Wales as a relatively stable maritime climate with mild winters (Jan min 3-4C) and moderate summers (July max 19-21C) thanks to Atlantic exposure.
properties.primary_vulnerability_signal: intense hourly rainfall and flash-flood risk
properties.trajectory_notes: Hourly rainfall extremes above 20 mm/h projected to become roughly 4x more frequent by 2070s under high emissions; +1.2 to 1.8C mean warming by mid-century.
properties.source: Met Office UKCP18; PMC9992391 (2023); World Bank CCKP UK
properties.gaps: Altitude range not specified; coastal cliff versus inland buffering not differentiated.

properties.buffering_notes: Climate.md identifies Mont Lozere, Aigoual, and Mejean plateau as elevation-mediated climate refugia where mean July maxima at 1500 m remain ~22-24C even under +2C global warming.
properties.primary_vulnerability_signal: cevenol autumn flash-flood storms with debris-flow risk
properties.trajectory_notes: Southern France warming above the national +1.7 to 2.7C trajectory; maximum daily autumn precipitation up ~30% since mid-20th century; summer precipitation projected down 15 to 25% by 2050.
properties.source: Meteo-France DRIAS 2024; WorldClim CMIP6 v2.1
properties.gaps: Lower-slope versus high-plateau microclimate boundaries not mapped quantitatively.

properties.buffering_notes: Climate.md describes a 1000-metre altitudinal gradient as a climate-selection lever; a settlement at 1000 m today experiences a climate similar to the Bolzano valley floor of 1950.
properties.primary_vulnerability_signal: glacier-fed irrigation baseflow loss and debris flows
properties.trajectory_notes: Alpine warming roughly twice the global mean; Ortles-Cevedale and Stubai glaciers lost over 30% of 1980 ice volume by 2024; more debris flows and convective storms since 2000.
properties.source: WorldClim CMIP6 v2.1 (2024); Bucchignani et al. Scientific Data 2023; Crespi et al. Scientific Reports 2020; EURAC
properties.gaps: Valley-inversion frequency and cold-air-pooling sites not enumerated.

properties.buffering_notes: Climate.md frames Asturias as buffered against extreme heat by Atlantic maritime influence with an abrupt coast-to-Cordillera-Cantabrica gradient and IPCC-recognised cool-temperate refugia above 1000 m.
properties.primary_vulnerability_signal: intense Atlantic storms and AMOC-shift tail risk
properties.trajectory_notes: Projected +1.5 to 2.0C by mid-century; Atlantic regime moderates the broader Spanish 14 to 20% rainfall decline projected for 2050.
properties.source: WorldClim CMIP6 v2.1 (2024); AEMET 2023; MDPI Land 14:85 (2025)
properties.gaps: Specific upper altitude limit for habitable buffering not stated.

properties.buffering_notes: Climate.md flags the central loess plain as exposed within Germany's primary inland-drought zone (Mitteldeutsches Trockengebiet); only the Harz foothills offer some microclimate variability.
properties.primary_vulnerability_signal: summer drought and groundwater drawdown
properties.trajectory_notes: Projected +1.8 to 2.5C by mid-century with pronounced drying trend; 2018, 2019, 2020, and 2022 summers already caused major agricultural impacts.
properties.source: WorldClim CMIP6 v2.1 (2024); DWD; PMC11322161 (2024); World Bank CCKP Germany
properties.gaps: Harz-foothill altitude buffering not quantified; only flagged qualitatively.

properties.buffering_notes: Climate.md notes Saaremaa coastal sites are 2-3C warmer in winter than mainland southeast due to Baltic moderation, with less snow but more wind-storm exposure.
properties.primary_vulnerability_signal: shrinking winter snow regime and Baltic Sea ecosystem shifts
properties.trajectory_notes: Northern latitudes amplify warming; +2.0 to 2.5C by mid-century, with Baltic SST projected +2 to 4C by 2100; growing season expands 2 to 4 weeks.
properties.source: WorldClim CMIP6 v2.1 (2024); Estonian Weather Service; World Bank CCKP Estonia; PMC4447695 (2015)
properties.gaps: Estonia is low-altitude with no mountain buffering; dossier does not give an altitude range.

properties.buffering_notes: Climate.md cites NCA5 projection that the Pacific Northwest will have the fewest very-hot days of any contiguous US region through 2060 because western Cascadia marine airflow buffers inland heat.
properties.primary_vulnerability_signal: late-summer wildfire smoke and heat-dome tail risk
properties.trajectory_notes: Projected +1.5 to 2.0C by mid-century; 2021 heat dome (Portland 46.7C) attribution studies indicate such events become 2-3x more likely by 2060; AQI above 150 days increasing.
properties.source: WorldClim CMIP6 v2.1 (2024); NCA5 Chapter 24; PRISM 1991-2020; Philip et al. WWA 2022
properties.gaps: Willamette Valley altitude band not given numerically.

properties.buffering_notes: Climate.md describes Vermont as wet, green, and free of heat-stress extremes; the design-relevant risk is precipitation intensity rather than thermal collapse.
properties.primary_vulnerability_signal: flash flooding on river-bottomlands per Irene 2011 and 2023 events
properties.trajectory_notes: NCA5 names the Northeast as the fastest-warming region in the contiguous US; +1.7 to 2.5C by mid-century with heat-index above 38C tripling by mid-century.
properties.source: NOAA NCEI 1991-2020; Vermont Climate Assessment 2021 (UVM Gund); NCA5 Chapter 21; WorldClim CMIP6 v2.1
properties.gaps: Specific altitude buffering bands for the Green Mountains not enumerated in climate.md.

properties.buffering_notes: Climate.md credits Asheville's ~670 m elevation for holding the baseline MAT to 13.5C and notes Appalachian highland zones track the lower end of the regional warming range due to elevation buffering; ridges shelter from Gulf tropical systems.
properties.primary_vulnerability_signal: extreme-precipitation flash flooding per Tropical Storm Helene 2024
properties.trajectory_notes: Projected +1.5 to 2.0C mean warming by mid-century; extreme-heat days above 31.8C projected to rise from ~7/yr to ~45/yr by 2050; annual precipitation rising from ~940 to ~1215 mm.
properties.source: WorldClim CMIP6 v2.1 (2024); NCA5 Chapter 22; NOAA NCEI; NC Climate Office; ClimateCheck 2025
properties.gaps: Upper altitude range and ridge-specific buffering not quantified.

properties.buffering_notes: Climate.md notes the karst and deep-coulee topography handles intense rain events better than flat tilled land but does not describe explicit thermal-buffering features.
properties.primary_vulnerability_signal: precipitation volatility with more intense rain interspersed with dry spells
properties.trajectory_notes: WICCI projects +2.3C statewide warming by 2041-2060 with 90F+ days rising from 9 to 26 per year; trajectory called the most benign in the framework but still substantial.
properties.source: Wisconsin State Climatology Office; WICCI 2024; NCA5 Great Lakes Chapter; WorldClim CMIP6 v2.1
properties.gaps: Coulee-versus-ridge thermal differential not quantified; no altitude range given.

properties.buffering_notes: Climate.md states ridgeline and plateau sites at 500-700 m run 3-5C cooler than valley floors in summer, extending the comfortable working season.
properties.primary_vulnerability_signal: intensifying dry-spell to flood cycle with diminishing summer baseflow
properties.trajectory_notes: Projected +2.0 to 2.5C above 1991-2020 by mid-century, pushing the region firmly into humid subtropical territory; days above 38C roughly doubling.
properties.source: NOAA NCEI 1991-2020; NWS Tulsa; NOAA NCICS Arkansas 2022; NCA5 Chapter 22; KSMU 2025
properties.gaps: Specific ridge versus valley microclimate maps not in dossier.

properties.buffering_notes: Climate.md frames the 2100 m elevation as the region's greatest climate asset and also its greatest illusion, currently below physiological heat danger but eroding fast.
properties.primary_vulnerability_signal: landscape-scale wildfire per Hermits Peak/Calf Canyon 2022
properties.trajectory_notes: New Mexico warmed 1.85C between 1970 and 2018, the fastest of any contiguous US state; NCA5 projects NM annual temperatures to most likely exceed historical record levels by mid-century even under lower emissions.
properties.source: WorldClim CMIP6 v2.1 (2024); NCA5 Chapter 2; Sillmann et al. ESEv 2022; 350newmexico.org
properties.gaps: Sangre de Cristo upper altitude limits for habitable buffering not exhaustively given.

properties.buffering_notes: Climate.md identifies the Atlantic Ocean as regulating temperature year-round, keeping the feels-like summer ceiling substantially lower than continental interior comparators like Ottawa or Montreal.
properties.primary_vulnerability_signal: intensifying noreasters, ice storms, and emerging wildfire risk
properties.trajectory_notes: Provincial projection of +2.6C by mid-century lifting MAT toward 9.3C; 2023 Upper Tantallon and 2025 fires were the province's most destructive on record.
properties.source: Nova Scotia Government Changing Climate (2023-2024); WorldClim CMIP6 v2.1; ClimateData.ca; ECCC normals
properties.gaps: Cape Breton Highlands altitude band quantified only as 1-2C colder than provincial mean.

properties.buffering_notes: Climate.md calls the altitudinal gradient the region's most valuable climate-adaptation lever; a settlement at 900 m today experiences roughly what the valley floor experienced in the 1970s.
properties.primary_vulnerability_signal: mega-fire season and shrinking glacial summer baseflow
properties.trajectory_notes: Columbia Basin projected 2.5 to 3.5C warmer than 1960s by 2050s; 2023 BC fire season burned over 2.84 million ha (more than double any prior year) with Drought Level 5 in the Kootenays.
properties.source: ClimateNA / AdaptWest CMIP6 SSP2-4.5; Nelson Climate Profile 2022; ClimateData.ca; BC Wildfire Service 2023
properties.gaps: Glacier-loss timing relative to settlement horizons not fully resolved.

properties.buffering_notes: Climate.md frames Estrie as one of northeastern North America's most livable climate zones, with reliable snowpack feeding spring soil moisture across rolling Appalachian terrain, but no explicit mountain-shelter or coastal-moderation feature.
properties.primary_vulnerability_signal: rising flash-flood risk from precipitation intensity
properties.trajectory_notes: Ouranos projects +2 to 3C for southern Quebec under SSP3-7.0 with SSP2-4.5 at the low end (~2C); frost-free period lengthens 2 to 3 weeks; growing season net-positive for regen operations.
properties.source: Environment Canada 1981-2010 Normals; Ouranos CMIP6 / ESPO-G6-R2; WorldClim CMIP6 v2.1
properties.gaps: Altitude band of the southern Appalachian foothills not numerically given.

properties.buffering_notes: Climate.md calls altitude the primary site-selection variable; a Sierra Norte settlement at 1800-2500 m gains 2-4C of thermal buffer and remains temperate through 2050 under SSP2-4.5, arguably the most climate-resilient band in the North American regen landscape.
properties.primary_vulnerability_signal: intensifying dry season requiring storage and fog-harvest
properties.trajectory_notes: South-southeast Mexico projected +1.5 to 2.0C by 2041-2060 with dry-season (March-May) temperatures warming fastest at +1.8C; Oaxaca named among states with most high-exposure municipalities.
properties.source: Vazquez-Aguirre et al. MDPI Climate 11(5):111 (2023); WorldClim CMIP6 v2.1; World Bank CCKP Mexico
properties.gaps: Cloud-forest fog-belt elevation not quantified beyond night-frost above 2500 m.
